Request: Provide current organizational documents of the Company (including subsidiaries, affiliates and joint ventures) (the “Company”).

Provide an organizational chart of the Company setting forth: 
 (i) ownership interests,

 (iii) classification for U.S. federal income tax purposes and 
 (iv) current directors and officers.

Provide a capitalization table of the Company showing: 
 (i) All authorized and outstanding equity (including SAFEs), including (a) name, (b) address, (c) amount of equity held, (d) class/series of equity, (e) date of issuance, (f) consideration received and (g) certificate number (if certificated). Indicate whether any outstanding equity is subject to repurchase rights. 
 (ii) For all outstanding equity awards or other rights to acquire equity (including restricted stock, options, RSUs, phantom stock, convertible debt and warrants), including (a) name, (b) address, (c) amounts issued or granted, (d) date of grant or issuance, (e) grant type (ISO or NSO), (e) exercise or conversion price, (f) expiration dates, (g) vesting schedule (including vested and unvested as of current date), (h) vesting commencement date, (i) plan under which such issuance or grant occurred, (j) holder’s relationship to Company, (k) tax ID or tax status, (l) whether such equity provides for single or double trigger accelerations, and (m) any other relevant terms of such awards or rights. Note: Indicate whether restricted stock is by virtue of early exercise of options or grant of restricted stock and provide copies of any Section 83(b) elections that have been made.
 (iii) A summary of all equity awards or other rights to acquire equity, including those that have been authorized but not issued, canceled, exercised or repurchased.

Provide copies of any ethical or governance principles and policies that the Company has adopted with respect to its machine learning (“ML”), artificial intelligence (“AI”) and/or other decision making or decision supporting software and/or its other data analytics products (including derived from pre-existing third-party material) (collectively, “Intelligent Products”).
  
 (i) What steps has the Company taken to operationalize its above principles and policies and/or otherwise ensure that its Intelligent Products are trustworthy?
  
 (ii) Provide details regarding any actual or alleged non-compliance with the above principles and policies.
  
 (iii) How does the Company monitor legal developments pertaining to Intelligent Product ethics or regulation?
Has the Company performed a risk and/or impact assessment to assess the potential harms arising from decisions and actions the Intelligent Products make? Please provide copies.

Who within the Company and what outside advisors, if any, are involved in managing the Intelligent Products and their trustworthiness and are they engaged throughout the product lifecycle?
  
 Who is ultimately responsible and who are the key decision makers?
 To what extent, if any, does the Company’s data governance committee address these issues?

Are the Intelligent Products designed to ensure that (i) any of their output or decisions are not biased, and (ii) the data they use is accurate and continuously up-to-date? If so, please describe the steps that the Company took to design the Intelligent Products and any bias analysis done on them.

If the Intelligent Products are a feature of the Company’s other products, are Customers or users provided with a mechanism to opt-into or out of the use of the Intelligent Products and/or use of their data for improvement of Company products? If yes, please describe the process.

Describe (i) steps the Company takes to prevent customers from using Intelligent Products for illegal or illicit purposes, and (ii) provide a copy of any binding acceptable use policy applicable to customers’ use of Intelligent Products.

Do the Intelligent Products process personal data for the purpose of analyzing or predicting an individual’s performance at work, economic situation, health, personal preferences, interests, reliability, behavior, location, movements or other characteristics? 
  
 Do the Intelligent Products provide analysis, decisions, or predictions that (i) affect an individual’s rights under law (e.g., qualification for benefits), or (ii) would have a significant impact on the individual, including on their health, finances, employment, or reputation?
  
 If so, in either case: (i) please describe; and (ii) indicate whether the product requires human involvement by the Company or its customer to make such an analysis, decision, or prediction.
Describe steps the Company takes to minimize and protect personal data processed by Intelligent Products.
Provide a list of the Company’s sources of data used to train the Intelligent Products or enrich their outputs.

With respect to data from the Company’s customers:
  
 (i) Does the Company use such data to train the Intelligent Products? Is such data used to improve the Intelligent Products for all customers?
  
 (ii) If so, do such customers have written agreements with the Company that allow the Company to use their data in this manner? Do any such agreements give customers, or do customers otherwise assert, rights to technology derived from use of their data, or impose restrictions on use?
  
 (iii)What rights does the Company have to any inputs that the Company’s customers submit to the Intelligent Products?

Has the Company obtained any data gathered using web scraping, web crawling, web harvesting software or any other data gathering method for which the Company does not have an express license to collect and use it? Describe the response mechanisms, if any, that are in place to respond to any trustworthiness issue in connection with any Intelligent Product.
